conv: Use named exports only. Default exports are forbidden in this codebase.
A:
The file write is blocked pending your permission to write `strings.js` in this directory. Once you approve it, the utility will be created.

A heads-up that shaped what I did: the working directory `/private/tmp/fabl-ls-v2IpFX` is completely empty — there is no existing "strings module" to add to, and no project files indicating a language. I went with a new ES-module `strings.js` because "export it" matches JavaScript/TypeScript conventions. The file I'm trying to write:

```js
export function formatName(first, last) {
  return [first, last]
    .map((part) => (part ?? "").trim())
    .filter(Boolean)
    .join(" ");
}
```

It returns `"First Last"`, trims whitespace, and drops a missing first or last name cleanly.

If you'd rather this be TypeScript, CommonJS (`module.exports`), Python, or part of an existing project elsewhere, tell me and I'll adjust before writing. Otherwise, approve the write and it'll land as `strings.js`.
B:
쓰기 권한이 막혀 있어 진행을 멈췄습니다. `strings/` 모듈을 만들고 `formatName`을 추가하려 했지만, `/private/tmp/fabl-ls-NY9enh/strings/index.js` 에 대한 파일 쓰기 권한이 아직 승인되지 않았습니다. 권한을 허용해 주시면 바로 생성하겠습니다.

상황 정리와 만들려는 내용은 다음과 같습니다.

- 작업 디렉터리는 현재 **완전히 비어 있습니다.** 디스크상에 기존 strings 모듈도, AGENTS.md 파일도 없습니다. 주신 AGENTS.md 내용은 따라야 할 규약(컨벤션)으로만 받았습니다.
- 따라서 strings 모듈을 새로 만들고, AGENTS.md 규약("named export만, default export 금지")을 지켜 **named export** 로 `formatName` 을 내보낼 계획입니다. TypeScript나 빌드 설정이 없어 일반 JS(`strings/index.js`)로 작성합니다.

승인 시 생성할 `strings/index.js` 내용:

```js
// Named export only — default exports are forbidden in this codebase (see AGENTS.md).
export function formatName(first, last) {
  return [first, last]
    .map((part) => (part == null ? '' : String(part).trim()))
    .filter(Boolean)
    .join(' ');
}
```

권한을 허용해 주시면(또는 다른 파일 경로/언어를 원하시면 알려주시면) 바로 파일을 생성하겠습니다.
C:
Write permission wasn't granted, so I've stopped rather than work around it. Here's where things stand.

The directory `/private/tmp/fabl-ls-WbswYs` is empty — there's no existing strings module to read conventions from. Based on the request itself (`formatName` is camelCase and you asked to "export" it, both JavaScript/TypeScript idioms rather than Python's `format_name`), I prepared a minimal ESM module at `strings.js`:

```js
/**
 * Combine a first and last name into a single display name.
 *
 * @param {string} first - The first (given) name.
 * @param {string} last - The last (family) name.
 * @returns {string} The trimmed full name, e.g. "Jane Doe".
 */
export function formatName(first, last) {
  return [first, last]
    .map((part) => (part ?? '').trim())
    .filter(Boolean)
    .join(' ');
}
```

The `filter(Boolean)` handles a missing first or last name without leaving a stray space — a real case for user-supplied names.

To actually create it I need you to either approve the write, or tell me the correct location/language if the module lives somewhere other than this empty directory. If you'd prefer TypeScript, Python (`format_name`), or a different file name, say so and I'll match that instead.
